channel.title: Java on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Java on Pi Stack

item.title: Flowable vs Activiti vs Camunda: Self-Hosted BPMN Engines Compared (2026)
item.description: Business Process Management (BPMN) engines are the backbone of enterprise workflow automation. They allow organizations to model, execute, and monitor complex business processes using the BPMN 2.0 standard. Whether you’re orchestrating approval workflows, handling case management, or building microservice choreography, a self-hosted BPMN engine gives you full control over your process data.

item.title: Apache Tomcat vs WildFly vs Gunicorn: Self-Hosted Application Servers 2026
item.description: When deploying web applications on your own infrastructure, choosing the right application server is critical. The application server sits between your code and the network, handling HTTP requests, managing connections, and executing your application logic. Three of the most widely used open-source options are Apache Tomcat, WildFly, and Gunicorn — each serving different ecosystems and use cases.

item.title: Drools vs OpenL Tablets vs Easy Rules: Self-Hosted Business Rules Engine Guide 2026
item.description: Business logic buried inside application code is one of the most common sources of technical debt. When pricing rules, compliance checks, eligibility criteria, or routing decisions are hardcoded, every change requires a full development cycle — code, test, build, deploy.
